View this page

Models¶

Overview¶

Use this page to browse the public model catalog, compare supported model implementations, and see how registered models are constructed through the shared PyHazards registry.

Model Catalog¶

The public catalog below is generated from pyhazards/model_cards/*.yaml. Use this page for model discovery and quick registry lookup. Core baselines, variants, and experimental adapters are listed separately so the public catalog does not over-count same-paper variants as independent methods. Use Coverage Audit for the audited roadmap gap list, Benchmarks for current runnable evaluator coverage, and the Implementation Guide for contributor workflow details.

Wildfire¶

Core Baselines¶

These entries count toward the current core public method set.

Model

Description

ASUFM

A temporal convolution baseline for weekly wildfire activity forecasting. See ASUFM repository.

DNN-LSTM-AutoEncoder

A two-stage wildfire framework with a DNN stage for incident-level cause and size prediction plus an LSTM + autoencoder stage for weekly forecasting. See Developing risk assessment framework for wildfire in the United States.

FireCastNet

A compact encoder-decoder baseline for wildfire spread mask prediction. See FireCastNet repository.

ForeFire Adapter

A lightweight simulator-style wildfire spread adapter inspired by front-propagation systems. See ForeFire repository.

wildfire_forecasting

A sequence forecasting baseline for next-window wildfire activity across weekly count features. See wildfire_forecasting repository.

WildfireSpreadTS

A temporal convolution wildfire spread baseline over short raster history windows. See WildfireSpreadTS repository.

WRF-SFIRE Adapter

A lightweight raster wildfire spread adapter inspired by WRF-SFIRE style transport. See WRF-SFIRE repository.

Variants and Additional Implementations¶

These entries stay public, but they are grouped outside the core method count because they are same-paper variants or additional off-plan implementations.

Model

Description

CNN-ASPP

An explainable CNN segmentation model with an ASPP mechanism for next-day wildfire spread prediction. See Application of Explainable Artificial Intelligence in Predicting Wildfire Spread.

Experimental Adapters¶

These entries remain public as lightweight wrapper or prototype integrations and should not be counted as stable core methods.

None.

Earthquake¶

Core Baselines¶

These entries count toward the current core public method set.

Model

Description

EQNet

A transformer-style earthquake phase-picking baseline for modern sequence modeling comparisons. See EQNet repository.

EQTransformer

A bidirectional sequence encoder for joint earthquake phase picking with attention pooling over waveform windows. See EQTransformer repository.

GPD

A compact CNN baseline for generalized phase detection and historical earthquake picking comparisons. See Generalized Phase Detection repository.

PhaseNet

A lightweight phase-picking baseline that predicts P- and S-arrival indices from multichannel waveform windows. See PhaseNet repository.

Variants and Additional Implementations¶

These entries stay public, but they are grouped outside the core method count because they are same-paper variants or additional off-plan implementations.

Model

Description

WaveCastNet

A ConvLEM-based sequence-to-sequence model for dense-grid earthquake wavefield forecasting and early-warning style rollout experiments. See Rapid wavefield forecasting for earthquake early warning via deep sequence to sequence learning.

Experimental Adapters¶

These entries remain public as lightweight wrapper or prototype integrations and should not be counted as stable core methods.

None.

Flood¶

Core Baselines¶

These entries count toward the current core public method set.

Model

Description

EA-LSTM

An entity-aware hydrology baseline with static-feature gating over streamflow histories. See NeuralHydrology repository.

FloodCast

A compact spatiotemporal flood-inundation baseline for raster forecast experiments. See FloodCast repository.

Google Flood Forecasting

A transformer-style sequence baseline for nodewise streamflow forecasting. See google-research/flood-forecasting repository.

NeuralHydrology LSTM

An adapter-style LSTM baseline for nodewise streamflow forecasting on graph-temporal inputs. See NeuralHydrology repository.

UrbanFloodCast

A U-Net style urban inundation baseline for dense-grid flood prediction. See UrbanFloodCast repository.

Variants and Additional Implementations¶

These entries stay public, but they are grouped outside the core method count because they are same-paper variants or additional off-plan implementations.

Model

Description

HydroGraphNet

A physics-informed graph neural network for flood forecasting with interpretable KAN-style components, residual message passing, and delta-state decoding. See Interpretable physics-informed graph neural networks for flood forecasting.

Experimental Adapters¶

These entries remain public as lightweight wrapper or prototype integrations and should not be counted as stable core methods.

None.

Hurricane¶

Core Baselines¶

These entries count toward the current core public method set.

Model

Description

Hurricast

A compact multimodal storm baseline for hurricane track and intensity forecasting. See Hurricast repository.

tropicalcyclone_MLP

A compact MLP baseline for hurricane track and intensity forecasting. See tropicalcyclone_MLP repository.

Variants and Additional Implementations¶

These entries stay public, but they are grouped outside the core method count because they are same-paper variants or additional off-plan implementations.

None.

Experimental Adapters¶

These entries remain public as lightweight wrapper or prototype integrations and should not be counted as stable core methods.

None.

Tropical Cyclone¶

Core Baselines¶

These entries count toward the current core public method set.

Model

Description

SAF-Net

A spatiotemporal tropical-cyclone baseline with an intensity-focused head and shared trajectory output. See TI_Prediction / SAF-Net repository.

TCIF-fusion

A knowledge-guided fusion baseline for tropical cyclone track and intensity forecasting. See TCIF-fusion repository.

TropiCycloneNet

A GRU plus attention baseline for all-basin tropical cyclone forecasting. See TropiCycloneNet repository.

Variants and Additional Implementations¶

These entries stay public, but they are grouped outside the core method count because they are same-paper variants or additional off-plan implementations.

None.

Experimental Adapters¶

These entries remain public as lightweight wrapper or prototype integrations and should not be counted as stable core methods.

Model

Description

FourCastNet TC Adapter

An experimental wrapper-style storm adapter inspired by FourCastNet forecast fields. See FourCastNet repository.

GraphCast TC Adapter

An experimental wrapper-style storm adapter inspired by GraphCast/GenCast forecast fields. See GraphCast / GenCast repository.

Pangu TC Adapter

An experimental wrapper-style storm adapter inspired by Pangu-Weather forecast fields. See Pangu-Weather repository.

Using the Registry¶

Build a Registered Model¶

from pyhazards.models import build_model

model = build_model(
    name="mlp",
    task="classification",
    in_dim=32,
    out_dim=5,
    hidden_dim=256,
    depth=3,
)

Register a Custom Model¶

Register a builder function that returns an nn.Module so users can load the model by name through the shared registry.

import torch.nn as nn
from pyhazards.models import register_model, build_model

def my_custom_builder(task: str, in_dim: int, out_dim: int, **kwargs) -> nn.Module:
    hidden = kwargs.get("hidden_dim", 128)
    layers = nn.Sequential(
        nn.Linear(in_dim, hidden),
        nn.ReLU(),
        nn.Linear(hidden, out_dim),
    )
    return layers

register_model("my_mlp", my_custom_builder, defaults={"hidden_dim": 128})

model = build_model(name="my_mlp", task="regression", in_dim=16, out_dim=1)

Notes¶

  • Builders receive task plus any kwargs you pass.

  • register_model stores optional defaults so configs can stay small.

  • Models are plain PyTorch modules and work with Trainer or custom loops.
